programme.title: Bloomberg The Opening Trade
programme.desc: "The Opening Trade" explores things to know as markets open across Europe; analysis that isn't available anywhere else, a break-down of the biggest stories of the day and top guests who have skin in the game.

programme.title: The Pulse with Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: Based in London, Francine Lacqua speaks with guests about global business, economics, finance and politics.

programme.title: Bloomberg Brief
programme.desc: "Bloomberg Brief" delivers the market news, data and analysis viewers need to set their agendas.

programme.title: Bloomberg Surveillance
programme.desc: Jonathan, Lisa and Annmarie survey the economy and global markets with daily conversations with leaders and decision makers, from Wall Street to Washington and beyond, to cover the latest in business, investment and geopolitics.

programme.title: Bloomberg The Asia Trade
programme.desc: Bringing global investors everything you need to know as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders.

programme.title: Bloomberg: The China Show
programme.desc: From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and David Ingles give global investors insight into the world's second-biggest economy, delivering in-depth discussions with newsmakers.

programme.title: Insight With Haslinda Amin
programme.desc: The definitive guide for global news audiences to get the complete picture on the story of the day, through probing interviews and nuanced conversations.

programme.title: Bloomberg Horizons Middle East & Africa
programme.desc: A spotlight on the fast paced growth and major deals happening across the Middle East and Africa, linking the market drivers from overnight in the U.S. and across Asia into the region's trading day, with deep-dive analysis of the top businesses.

programme.title: Bloomberg Daybreak: Europe
programme.desc: From London, essential morning viewing to stay ahead of the day's market trends with news stories from the U.S. and Asia in the lead-up to the opening of European markets.

programme.title: Life & Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: no info available

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Death for a Stolen Horse : Joe Riva is nearly lynched by Pete Hendell for horse stealing which is the punishment in Kansas. Earp stops the lynching and believes Riva is innocent.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Last Stand at Smoky Hill : Bat leads a group of hunters on a buffalo hunt believing huge herds have returned. When Earp learns the Apache have left their reservation to hunt them, he attempts to warn the group.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Hole Up : Wells Fargo asks Earp to go undercover into the badlands to capture Blackie Saunders wanted for a robbery and murder.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Peacemaker : While Earp is out of town bringing in Blackie Saunders against Kelley's orders, Curly and Ringgold take over Dodge City.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Bounty Killer : Col. Benteen asks Earp to help with a string of murders in the outlaw area called Robber's Roost. Earp agrees to investigate and finds himself working with outlaws.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Caught by a Whisker : Two brothers decide to rob the Dodge City State National Bank. However, a new alarm bell in the bank and a wise Earp expecting them pose problems for the men getting away.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Mysterious Cowhand : After Earp's cousin is killed with a knife, Earp decides to go alone to Texas disguised as a cowhand to find the killer.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Gatling Gun : General Sherman asks Earp to intervene with the Nez Perce tribe and Chief Joseph about reclaiming a Gatling gun stolen by Nez Perce.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Cattle Thieves : Earp and Judge Tobin are forced to enforce a rustling law they think is too strict.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: How to Be a Sheriff : The new sheriff learns the difficulties of the job when he is given papers to evict outlaw brothers.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Judas Goat : A Deputy US Marshal from Topeka asks Earp to help capture a gang of outlaws led by Rocky Griswold.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Doc Fabrique's Greatest Case : Shotgun's mule Roscoe is sick and he wants Dr. McCarty to help. When he says the mule is drunk, Earp decides to call Doc Fabrique from Wichita to help.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Actress : Actress Cora Campbell comes to Dodge City to perform creating interest among the men and especially silversmith Tom Tanner.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Love and Shotgun Gibbs : An agent for the Santa Fe railroad tells Earp that they expect a gang led by One Eyed Brown is going to rob the train.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Dodge Is Civilized : Mayor Kelley wants to close down the remaining saloons just as Bat returns to take over the Lone Star saloon.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Little Gray Home in the West : Earp harbors a desire to own a ranch so when Doc has a chance to win one in a game, Earp goes in on it.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Cyclone : Word is received by telegraph that a cyclone is coming toward Dodge City.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Kelley Was Irish : Mayor Kelley wanting law and order fires Earp and Shotgun after Doc shoots a gunman at de Graff's saloon.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Arizona Comes to Dodge : Morgan and Virgil Earp come to Dodge City to ask Wyatt to join them in Arizona where they plan to ranch.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Muleskinner : Earp's old friend Sam McGuffin stops in Dodge City with a load of freight and his six mules.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp Ain't Even Wearing Guns : Earp is goaded by a southern lady into hanging up his guns while her father's Texas crew is in Dodge City. He and Shotgun are forced to resort to their fists to control the outfit.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Bat Jumps the Reservation : Bat Masterson returns to Dodge City after a good streak of gambling with Cora Warrous and her brother.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: The Truth About Rawhide Geraghty : Earp is asked by Mrs. Geraghty to find out what is bothering her husband. Earp finds out he has one more stagecoach run before he retires but he is afraid to make it.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: She Almost Married Wyatt : A runaway girl is found by Mr. Cousin but when Earp investigates the family who adopted her, he decides she should not be returned to it.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Horse Race : A horse race between horse owner Milt Canyon and Bullhead turns violent when the Indian pony wins.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Juveniles : Earp arrests a boy wearing a gun trying to buy alcohol who looks to be 17 and looking for trouble.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: One Murder - Fifty Suspects : Loan shark Skinner Smith is found poisoned. Everyone in town hates Smith and wanted him dead giving Earp a large number of suspects.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: How to Be a Sheriff : The new sheriff learns the difficulties of the job when he is given papers to evict outlaw brothers.

programme.title: FBI Files
programme.desc: no info available

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Melissa Brannen: Missing : In 1989, residents of a close-knit apartment community in Virginia gathered for a holiday celebration. For Tammy Brannen and five-year-old daughter Melissa, Christmas was always a special time. Then without warning, the little girl was gone.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Fatal Friendship : In 1992, a woman disappeared from her Memphis home. When the ransom went uncollected, the FBI suspected a desperate amateur and feared the worst.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Ivy League Murder : Etna, New Hampshire is a small town. It's the kind of place where people trust their neighbors and leave their doors unlocked. A double murder shattered that trust and left the entire community terrified.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Independence Day Breakout : In 1987 the New Mexico Penitentiary housed hundreds of violent offenders, rapists, killers, men the juries put behind bars to protect society. On July fourth, seven of those men escaped.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Master Plan : Willow Springs, Illinois, seemed like any other quiet Midwestern community. But it was really one of the most corrupt places in the nation. This tiny village, nestled just outside of Chicago, was in the hip pocket of organized crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Pursuit : When law enforcement officers become targets, no one is safe. So when a crystal meth dealer on the run from the law began shooting cops, it took more than police to stop him.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Inside The Bureau : In this special episode, we take a closer look at cases previously featured on this series. Our goal is to explore more fully some of the investigative methods and techniques that have made the FBI the premier law enforcement agency in the world.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Death In The Delta : When a wealthy young woman was abducted in front of her in-laws' home, the police had no shortage of suspects. The 25-year-old victim left behind a tumultuous marriage, an ex-husband and old boyfriends. All were on the FBI's short list.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Evil Intent : Drops of blood and a torn screen were all that's left to tell authorities what happened to a woman in Indiana. The 21 yr. old victim was taken from her home on a spring morning. She appeared to have no real enemies, there was no clear motive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Betrayed : On the day before Thanksgiving, a mother discovered her ten-year-old son was missing from his school. When a child of separated parents disappears, investigators first look at the family and divorced parents often suspect each other.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Dishonored : Shirley Russell was determined enough to reach the rank of Captain in the United States Marine Corps. When this officer turned up missing, her colleagues refused to believe she had gone AWOL. It was unclear whether Captain Shirley Russell was alive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Price of Greed : An armed robbery is a terrifying ordeal. When a gang descended on a Los Angeles cash vault, they threatened to kill anyone who resisted. The violent assault was the largest heist in U.S. history and a piece of plastic was the only evidence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Shattered Shield : In a city awash in narcotics the drug lords have all the power even over the police. Corruption in New Orleans grew like cancer, eating away at public safety and threatening to destroy the city. Crooked cops break the laws they were sworn to uphold.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Vanished : A daring kidnapping and a bizarre ransom note baffled Mississippi authorities. The victim was the wife of a wealthy businessman. A list of 12 names and a few drops of blood were the only clues to her fate.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Blood Brothers : When the small community of Noel, Missouri discovered their bank was robbed, they're shocked. When the bank president turned up missing, they're outraged. And when clues began to surface the facts were more terrible than anyone could have guessed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Bitter End : In Las Vegas, a gang of robbers target an ATM repository and hit the jackpot. Their take was over a million dollars in cash, the largest heist in Las Vegas history.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Dangerous Gamble : They were a gang of criminal masterminds. They carried automatic weapons and were not afraid to use them. With each and every new heist, they became bolder and more deadly, until the FBI stepped in, determined to end their violent rampage.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: no info available

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Loved To Death : A victim usually never foresees danger when the perpetrator turns out to be a friend or a lover. Science and microscopic evidence can unmask these killers and find justice for those who are Loved To Death.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Material Witness : Lies and deceit can often throw investigators off the trail of justice. But when hard evidence contradicts a killer's story, police must use the clues to piece together the truth.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Family Plots : Every family has its secrets, and sometimes blood relations lead to bloodshed. When murder becomes a family affair, investigators must turn to forensics to uncover family plots.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: True Crime : They know as much about crime as any crime fighter, or any criminal. They're the crime writers, and through their eyes we see murder most foul.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Coroner Investigator : CI: Coroner Investigator will reveal the most in-depth look to date into the science of death.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Stranger Than Fiction : A millionaire is found dead, murdered for a stash of buried silver. A young woman dates violent men, only to be killed by her best friend.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Broken Trust : A New York homebuyer gets more than he bargained for when a house inspection turns up a mummified corpse. For decades, the crime had gone undiscovered. The victim unmissed, and the killer unpunished.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Trial By Fire : At a crime scene, anything left behind or seemingly out of place is considered a clue. But a fire can extinguish everything in its path, challenging forensic investigators at every turn and making each arson a trial by fire.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Deadly Chemistry : The show examines cases of poison and deadly chemistry and shows how forensic experts are solving mysterious deaths today and from the past.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Shreds of Evidence : Hairs and Fibers: The tiniest filament can become a mark of distinction in the most singular and intimate of ways. Investigators have come to rely on forensic evidence as fine as a carpet fiber or as innocent as an eyelash to crack difficult cases.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Trails of Evidence : In criminal investigations, a simple clue can provide the missing link by placing a suspect at a crime scene. Dirt left on shoes, tires or clothes, or even a tiny piece of plastic can pinpoint a crime scene.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Deadly intentions : When abduction turns to murder, forensic science is the only key to finding justice for the victims of a kidnapper's deadly intentions.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Remnant of Blame : The solution to the most heinous crimes often hinge on the smallest of clues. Investigators must have their eyes trained to find the full story of a murder written in a single scrap of evidence.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Soldier Stories : This show profiles the work of world-renowned forensic experts as they work to tell the stories of the young men who went to war (from the French and Indian war to Vietnam) and never came back.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Patterns Of Guilt : The tread of a tire, a single shoe print and even the shape of a bruise help investigators track down killers, based solely on their patterns of guilt.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Cold Cases : These cases took a decade or more to solve. There's no statute of limitations on murder. As a case turns cold, the clues become scarce, investigators must rely on science to close cold cases.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Vanished : In New York, an ambitious college student has her whole life ahead of her, until she crosses the path of a killer. It's a random murder, the hardest kind to solve.

programme.title: The New Detectives

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Eddie's Daughter : Eddie's daughter causes trouble for Lucas when two strangers threaten her life after she arrives with criminal information and stolen money.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Panic : Town character Brett Conway blackmails Lucas with the knowledge that a young couple Lucas rescued is suffering from yellow fever.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Ordeal : Lucas and Mark are stranded in the middle of a desert with very little food or water, and after Lucas is injured, Mark becomes the only hope for their survival.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Spiked Rifle : While trying to protect Banker Hamilton's gold, Mark is kidnapped and held hostage at the McCain ranch and will be killed within the hour unless Lucas cooperates.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Letter of the Law : Lucas makes a life-or-death decision when Micah is held hostage and the kidnappers demand the release of a notorious outlaw.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Legacy : Lucas tries to do a good deed for the old town drunk but it backfires when the drunk's son believes he inherited Lucas' ranch.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: The Baby Sitter : A dance hall singer asks Lucas to care for her daughter in an effort to hide the baby from her self-righteous, bigoted father.

programme.title: Annie Oakley
programme.desc: The Dutch Gunmaker : A Dutch gunsmith, the inventor of an new rifle with superior range, plans to give the weapon to the United States Army in gratitude for being allowed to immigrate.

programme.title: The Cisco Kid
programme.desc: The Will : Cisco and Pancho come across Bob Drake being chased by two outlaws. After helping him, Cisco hears his story of being framed for a gold robbery. Cisco believes Bob and decides to investigate.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: The Favor : Owing a favor to a sheriff, Josh agrees against better judgment to go to the lawless town of Bent Horn to bring back a man wanted for bank robbery and murder.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Ricochet : Josh sets out to help a heartbroken wife locate her missing husband, but his mission of mercy meets with a resistant and tight-lipped townspeople.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Sheriff of Red Rock : Josh is bushwhacked by bounty hunters in cahoots with the local sheriff, who now must keep Josh from blowing the whistle on their illegal but lucrative \\"bounty game\\".

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Die by the Gun : A sadistic pair of killers escape from prison who have no qualms about killing.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Rawhide Breed : Following an Indian attack on their stagecoach that kills his prisoner, Randall and an eastern gentleman unaccustomed to the ways of the West must trek to safety across many long, hot miles of hostile Apache territory.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Eight Cent Reward : On Christmas, Eve Randall receives his most daunting assignment yet from a young boy who has eight cents to pay: bring in Santa Claus.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Drop to Drink : Thieves are lying in wait to ambush mail carriers and eventually Josh is hired to help ensure that the mail will get through.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Rope Law : Josh is knocked out by a posse who wants his prisoner who is wanted for killing his step-daughter.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Six-Up to Bannach : Josh needs to get his wounded prisoner to Bannach in three days to prevent an innocent man from hanging.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: The Spurs : Josh rides into a small town looking for Vic Warsaw but is told by the Sheriff that Warsaw was buried that day and killed by Miss Lucy Fremont the night before.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Reunion for Revenge : Four men break out of prison looking for revenge against Aben Starr who turned them in after their first escape attempt.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Competition : Josh is resting in Benson when two bounty hunters bring in a man - the mayor - dead.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Call Your Shot : A grieving father who is the town drunk hires Josh Randall with his last $30 to find his murdered son's body, but Josh's search for clues runs up against a cowardly conspiracy of silence.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Secret Ballot : At the request of his friend's wife, Randall comes to assist and protect a war buddy in his bid for the town's mayoralty, quickly running afoul of the established political machine and its corrupt mayor and wanted sheriff.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: The Corner : A bounty hunter is not a hero in his own hometown, as Josh learns when he honors a dying friend's request to return his body to Cameron and the townspeople rush to judgment, believing Josh killed him for the reward money.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Eager Man : Josh is approached by the wife of a wanted man eager to turn himself in peacefully, provided Josh splits the bounty with his wife.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: The Legend : A man with unwavering faith in a legend about lost treasure hires Josh to lead his family on an expedition into the desert to locate the cache of Spanish gold.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Railroaded : When a young runaway is framed for killing a Pinkerton agent, Randall turns detective to uncover the real killer.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Double Fee : Josh is requested to meet a famous singer at the opera house in Denver who has a job for him.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: The Kovack Affair : Josh comes to the aid of his old friend Jesse, who is being strong-armed into selling the Outrider Hotel and Saloon owned by his beautiful girlfriend.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Bounty for a Bride : A decidedly different and dangerous assignment awaits Josh when he agrees to go into the Apache camp and compete in their contests to win the hand of a woman.

programme.title: Wanted: Dead or Alive
programme.desc: Crossroads : Pursuing killer Joe Collins into the Superstition Mountains, Josh is followed by a man who believes Collins could be his long-lost son.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Don't You Want Me, Pt. 1 : Alex and her mother Emily are in trouble - Chad, Emily's boyfriend, has run up thousands of dollars in credit card debt and hasn't been paying the rent.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Don't You Want Me, Pt. 2 : Alex's double life is finally bringing in the cash she wants but hiding everything from everyone is taking a toll on her schoolwork and her connection with Paige.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Standing In The Dark, Pt. 1 : The corridors of Degrassi have become a pressure cooker. With an unexpected influx of students from Lakehurst, Degrassi students find themselves facing old enemies and new friends.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Standing In The Dark, Pt. 2 : Abstinence-pledged Darcy wakes up in a strange bed, screamingly hung-over. Panic-stricken, she tries to piece together the events of the night before. She knows – physically – she had sex, but everything else is a blank.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Love is a Battlefield : Paige is ecstatic to have landed herself a gig in the fashion world. If everything goes well, this could turn itself into a brand new career path.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: It's Tricky : After seeing Craig’s newly released album on the shelves at the record store, Ashley decides to pursue her own music – starting with a performance at the Lakehurst-Degrassi talent mixer.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Death or Glory, Pt. 1 : Amidst the crowded classrooms and the battle for school territory, tensions between Lakehurst and Degrassi students continue to mount. But Spinner’s got his eye on enemy territory -- the adorably tough new Lakehurst girl, Jane.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Death or Glory, Pt. 2 : Faced with his own aggression and looming mortality, Spinner attempts to prove his ‘masculinity’ by kicking an unnecessary amount of Lakehurst ass.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: We Got The Beat : Manny’s moved back home but the honeymoon between she and her parents is just about over. And when her dad insists she have a traditional “Debut” – the lavish party many Filipino girls have for their 18th birthday – Manny takes a stand.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Money For Nothing : Mia’s T-Bombz campaign is hitting the city and Degrassi. But Holly J has bigger and better things going on. She’s prepping the Power Squad for an out-of-town competition, and things are looking good.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Lost in Love, Pt. 1 : Spinner and Jane have plans for next year – she’ll start pre-law and he’ll attend Police College. When he doesn’t get in, he can’t bear to tell her, so he lies.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Lost in Love, Pt. 2 : Desperate to win Jane back, Spinner sneaks into the Leadership Convention Jane is speaking at and proposes. When Jane turns him down, Spinner breaks up with her – nothing he does is ever good enough.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Bad Medicine : Riley’s the star of Degrassi’s first-ever football game win! The Shep is floored at how much Riley’s improved.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Causing A Commotion : Clare stands up for Connor when he receives unfair treatment from The Shep. The result of their peaceful protest? Detentions for everyone. No one is more frustrated by the detention than Alli. It wasn’t her idea to protest.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Heat of the Moment : Pushed to the edge by Holly J, Alli begins posting nasty things about her on the “anti-Grapevine” blog, the underground school on-line site.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Jane Says, Pt. 1 : The Shep has returned after his sensitivity training and it’s Spirit Week at Degrassi! However spirits are anything but high at home for Jane as she struggles to get her dropout brother Lucas off the couch.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Jane Says, Pt. 2 : Jane admits to herself that her issues with her dad go deeper than the divorce – she thinks maybe he did things to her. Jane is very confused and feels alone. Lucas gives Jane a hard time for creating tension between mom and dad.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Touch of Grey : Emma’s tired of being referred to as Kelly’s girlfriend and Blonde Emma – she’s not even blonde anymore! Determined to create a distinct university identity, Emma borrows some pot that was gifted to Kelly and smokes up with Becca and Gwyneth.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Heart of Glass : Alli wants Johnny to like her, a lot. She worries she’s going to lose him to an older more “experienced” girl so to make sure she keeps her man close, Alli decides to play with the big kids at a ravine party.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Up Where We Belong : Stress is at an all-time high at Degrassi with exams approaching and course selections due for next term.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Danger Zone : Blue asks Holly J to the winter dance in a very romantic way. Only problem? Holly J thinks she might have a thing for her co-worker, Spinner. While Blue is a dreamboat, Spinner is a goofball.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Paradise City, Pt. 1 : Kevin Smith and Jason Mewes are back! But this time, the Degrassi gang ventures to L.A. via school bus to score parts in Mewesical High, a fictional musical directed by Jason Mewes.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Paradise City, Pt. 2 : Kevin Smith and Jason Mewes are back! But this time, the Degrassi gang ventures to L.A. via school bus to score parts in Mewesical High, a fictional musical directed by Jason Mewes.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Paradise City, Pt. 3 : Kevin Smith and Jason Mewes are back! But this time, the Degrassi gang ventures to L.A. via school bus to score parts in Mewesical High, a fictional musical directed by Jason Mewes.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Paradise City, Pt. 4 : Kevin Smith and Jason Mewes are back! But this time, the Degrassi gang ventures to L.A. via school bus to score parts in Mewesical High, a fictional musical directed by Jason Mewes.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Just Can't Get Enough, Pt. 1 : When Mia is offered a MAJOR modeling contract in Paris, Peter decides he’ll go with her. But he soon realizes he doesn’t fit into her sophisticated world.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Just Can't Get Enough, Pt. 2 : After Peter discovers his recreational drug was not-so-recreational meth, he swears he’ll never touch the stuff again. But with the reality of coming down, Peter realizes he just wants more.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Close To Me : Jane and Spinner have been through so much together. They’re best friends. Jane’s just come out of a difficult year at home, so hanging out with Spinner is safe and… easy. That’s not a bad thing, right?

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Wanna Be Startin' Somethin' : Jane and Holly J team up for a practicum business assignment and immediately bond over their idea to start a babysitting agency.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation

programme.desc: Somebody : Against all odds, Holly J has fallen for Declan. Suddenly she gets what everyone’s been so excited about for so long.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Did Blue See? : It's Blue's Clues from a whole new perspective! We play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue saw. Along the way, we see things through mirrors and kaleidoscopes, from up above and down below.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Nurture! : We help Steve and Blue take care of their plants, and now there's something else that we need to do. We play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue is Frustrated : Something is frustrating Blue, so we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is. We learn to "stop, take a deep breath, and think" when we find ourselves in frustrating situations.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Is Blue Trying To Do? : Blue wants to show us something she's been trying to do, so we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Mechanics! : We help Steve and Blue fix their broken toy plane. There's something else to fix, so we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Art Appreciation : We are having a big art show and we play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue is making for the show.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Weight and Balance : We play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue wants to do. Throughout the day, with the help of our scale friends, we compare the weight of objects and determine which are heavy and which are light.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Geography : We play Blue's Clues to figure out where Blue wants to go. Using a map, we help Shovel and Pail figure out which buildings would be best suited for specific errands then we decide what form of transportation would work best.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What's that Sound? : Blue hears something that we can't hear, so we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is. Led by our ears, we help Shovel and Pail follow the path a gopher took in the woods, based on the sounds he makes.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What's New, Blue? : What's New, Blue?: Steve and Blue are inspired to create a newspaper to tell everyone what's new around the house. Ee play Blue's Clues to help Steve search for news.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Bugs! : Bugs!: To the untamed wilderness of our backyard we go! Join Steve and Blue on a bug-watching expedition and help to play Blue's Clues as we figure out what Blue's favorite bug is.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: ¡Un Día Con Plum! : ¡Un Día Con Plum!: A visit from Plum gets us learning new Spanish words. Meanwhile, we play Blue's Clues to figure out what kind of marioneta Blue thinks Plum and Periwinkle should make.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Making Changes : Making Changes: The arrival of a new baby in the house means making some adjustments. We play Blue's Clues to figure out an additional way to assist Salt and Pepper in taking care of Cinnamon

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Something To Do Blue : Something To Do Blue: Blue can't decide what to do today. Mr. Salt encourages us to pick a few items from around the house and use our imaginations to invent something new to do with them.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue's Book Nook : Blue's Book Nook: Blue is organizing the books in her book nook. We play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue wants to read about.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Let's Boogie : Let's Boogie: Steve's got sneakers on today and that makes him feel like dancing! We play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue wants to make a dance about.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blocks : Blocks: Blue's friend Green is over today and we're playing with blocks. Steve is making a playground for his cow figurines and Blue is building a really tall tower.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue's School : Hop on board our cardboard bus and help Steve pick up our friends for a day of pretend school, where Blue is the teacher! We play Blue's Clues to figure out where we'll be going.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Rhyme Time : Rhyme Time: If you've got a rhyme, we've got the time today, Steve is a poet and he knows it - and so are we! We play Blues Clues to figure out what Blue wants to rhyme about.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue's Backyard Ballgame Bonanza : Blue's Backyard Ballgame Bonanza: If it's round and rolls, there's a good chance it belongs in Blue's Backyard Ballgame Bonanza. We're inventing all sorts of new ballgames today.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: The Snack Chart : Today we're using charts to measure all sorts of things. Mr. Salt needs to figure out what everyone's favorite snack is so he can make them, so we make a chart to help him out!

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Colors Everywhere! : Today we're painting portraits of our friends. We play Blue's Clues to figure out who else Blue wants to add to our Portrait of Pals.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Shape Searchers : Joe and Blue are playing Shape Searchers and they need our help. Blue pulls various shapes out of her bag and we look around and find things with the same shape!

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Surprise Guest : It's time to clean up because a special guest is coming over to visit! But who is it? We play Blue's Clues to figure out who our surprise guest is. While searching for clues, we straighten up the house.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Contraptions : We're making contraptions! Joe demonstrates his amazing "Crayon Slide" for us. Blue is making a contraption, too, and we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it's going to do.

programme.title: Bubble Guppies
programme.desc: Build Me a Building! : It’s going to be the best doghouse EVER! But before Bubble Puppy can move into his new doggy digs, the Bubble Guppies have to build it. Help them build it while discovering all about construction.

programme.title: Bubble Guppies
programme.desc: Grumpfish Special : At the Bubble Guppies' restaurant, the group is waiting tables, cooking food,and preparing to serve their most difficult customer yet. But is there anything on the menu that can get the Grumpfish grinning?

programme.title: Bubble Guppies
programme.desc: We Totally Rock! : Are you ready to rock? The Bubble Guppies are taking the stage with their guitars, drums and keyboards. Get ready to crank it up to 10 and learn about everything rock and roll.

programme.title: Bubble Guppies
programme.desc: Call a Clambulance : When Oona’s friend Avi breaks a bone in his tail, he needs to visit the doctor. The Bubble Guppies aren’t too worried – after they learn that it’s a doctor’s job to make everyone feel better.

programme.title: Bubble Guppies
programme.desc: The Legend of Pinkfoot! : The Bubble Guppies are going on a camping trip! Grab your flashlights and gather around the campfire to make s'mores and hear about the spooky legend of Pinkfoot!

programme.title: Bubble Guppies
programme.desc: Bubble Puppy! : Gil wants to adopt a puppy! But before he can bring the puppy home, the Bubble Guppies must learn how to take care of pets.

programme.title: Bubble Guppies
programme.desc: Can You Dig It? : Rawrrrrrr! While the Bubble Guppies are digging for dinosaur bones, they learn all about different types of dinosaurs.

programme.title: Bubble Guppies
programme.desc: Super Shrimptennial Celebration! : To celebrate Shrimptennial, the Bubble Guppies visit Big Bubble City! Join them in discovering the hustle and bustle of the big city and the Big Bubble Building!

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Friends, Romans, and Accountants : Norm asks Diane to fill in as his bosses' date for the firm's annual bash.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Truce or Consequences : Sam steps in as a mediator between the bickering Carla and Diane.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Coach Returns to Action : Coach feels he can't compete with Sam when they fall for the same woman.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Endless Slumper : Sam loans out his lucky cap to a baseball player who's in a slump.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: One for the Book : Inebriated for the first time in his life, a timid young priest-to-be makes an amorous advance toward Diane and concludes he's not fit for the monastery.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: The Spy Who Came in for a Cold One : Diane incurs the wrath of Sam and the entire Cheers clan when she exposes a customer who spouts captivating, tall-tales of his exploits as a World War II spy.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Now Pitching, Sam Malone : A casting agent pursues Sam, offering more than just a business proposition.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Let Me Count the Ways : Saddened by the death of her cat, Diane gains little sympathy from the Cheers clan and begins to reevaluate her relationships with everyone, especially Sam.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Father Knows Last : Diane is aghast when a very pregnant Carla tricks the surprised, but ecstatic egghead Marshall Lipton into believing he's the father.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Cliff's Rocky Moment : A man in the bar becomes sick of Cliff's obnoxious know-it-all behavior and challenges him to a fight. Will Cliff shut up, accept the challenge, or run away?

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Fortune and Men's Weight : Coach brings fortunes to Cheers when he buys an antique scale.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Snow Job : Diane forbids Sam to go on an annual ski trip with his buddies.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Coach Buries a Grudge : Coach is hurt when he learns that his deceased friend once propositioned his wife.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norman's Conquest : Norm tries to prove his manliness when his new client makes advances.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: I'll Be Seeing You (Part 1) : Sam hires a brilliant artist to paint Diane as a peace offering.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: I'll Be Seeing You (Part 2) : Diane defends her relationship with Sam to a slandering artist.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebound (Part 1) : In the aftermath of their explosive separation, Sam has fallen off the wagon with a thud and has returned to his playboy ways, while Diane has just returned from a self-imposed rest at Golden Brook Sanitarium.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebound (Part 2) : After several sessions with Dr. Frasier Crane, Sam is getting his life back in order, so Diane feels it's time to tell him the truth about her relationship with the psychiatrist.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: I Call Your Name : Sam gloats when he has reason to believe that Diane is still in love with him.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Fairy Tales Can Come True : Frasier asks Sam to escort Diane to an event while he is out of town.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam Turns The Other Cheek : Sam reaps the consequences of getting involved with a married woman.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Coach In Love (Part 1) : Coach proposes marriage when he falls in love at first sight.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Coach In Love (Part 2) : After Coach's girlfriend Irene wins the lottery, she becomes a socialite and breaks up with him.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane Meets Mom : Frasier's mom threatens Diane's life when they meet for the first time.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: An American Family : Carla's ex-husband returns with his new bride, demanding custody of their oldest son. The normally feisty Carla shocks everyone at the bar when she agrees to the request.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane's Allergy : After moving in with Frasier, Diane develops a chronic allergy and believes his beloved dog, Pavlov, causes it.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Peterson Crusoe : Norm decides to live life to the fullest when he gets back his medical test results.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: A Ditch In Time : At the bar, Sam's innocent flirtation with an attractive customer, Amanda, gets him into trouble when he doesn't realize she's a member of Diane's therapy group.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Whodunit : Carla wins the favor and admiration of Frasier's esteemed mentor.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: The Heart Is A Lonely Snipe Hunter : The guys pull a cruel joke on Frasier while they're on a fishing trip.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: King Of The Hill : Sam volunteers to play in a charity softball game against Playboy Playmates.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Teacher's Pet : Sam gets Coach's help to help him pass a geography class and get his high school diploma. But he eventually finds an easier way to pass the course.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Y'en a Dans Le Ventilo : La série Cop Drama va diffuser un épisode ou le mot 'Merde' sera prononcé. Dès lors une épidémie de peste se déclare.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Combat d'Infirme : Al Super Gay est viré des scouts parce qu'il est gay. Et les enfants font la connaissance de Jimmy Valmer, un handicapé qui prend la vie avec humour. Mais Timmy est jaloux du succès de Jimmy, et un combat d'infirmes est engagé.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Terrance et Phillip : de tempêtes en naufrages : Terrence et Phillip, les canadiens pétomanes, mettent fin à leur duo pour divergences créatives. Les garçons se mettent en tête de réunir leurs idoles.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman Land : Suite à la mort de sa grand-mère, Cartman hérite d'un million de dollars et réalise son rêve de toujours: il achète un parc d'attraction (dont il profite tout seul) qu'il rebaptise 'Cartmanland '.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Du bon usage du preservatif : Les parents découvrent que leurs enfants sont très mal informés sur les choses du sexe. On demande à l'école de donner des cours. À croire que les parents ignorent qui sont les enseignants, Mme Crockelpaf, Mr Mackey et Mr Garrison.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Servietsky : À peine les enfants ont-ils obtenu leur console Gamesphère qu'on leur enlève. Ils doivent livrer une serviette intelligente fumant des pétards a une organisation militaire. Seulement les gamins se retrouvent embrouillés dans une histoire.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Oussama Ben Laden pue du cul : Kenny, Cartman, Stan et Kyle sont envoyés par erreur en Afghanistan. Sur place et contre toute attente, ils vont s'apercevoir qu'ils ont beaucoup de points communs avec les enfants Afghans.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Comment manger avec son cul : Cartman placarde la photo de Kenny associée à la mention 'Enfant disparu' sur tous les packs de lait du pays.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: L'Engin : Mr Garrison, ne supportant plus que ses vols d'avions soient annulés ou retardés, décide d'inventer un nouveau moyen de transport.Kyle est très embarrassé quand il reçoit la visite de son cousin de New-York, également prénommé Kyle.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Les riches debarquent : Se sentant rejeté, Token décide d'appeler d'autres riches. Mais les hommes de la ville ne supportent pas l'arrivée de ces riches.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Rock chrétien : Suite à un pari avec Kyle, Cartman décide de monter un groupe de rock chrétien avec Token et Butters, dans le but d'obtenir un disque de platine.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Rencontre du troisième âge : À South Park, comme dans tout le pays, les vieux au volant sont considérés comme un fléau. Une loi est votée pour leur confisquer leur permis de conduire. Mais le troisième âge contre-attaque.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Casa Bonita : La mère de Kyle doit emmener ce dernier et ses amis à Casa Bonita, une version mexicaine de Disneyland au Colorado. Au lieu d'inviter Cartman, Kyle lui préfère Butters.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Tout sur les Mormons : Un enfant mormon arrive dans la classe des garçons. Stan est supposé lui casser la figure, mais son père et lui sont soudain fascinés par la religion des nouveaux venus, et leur extrême gentillesse. Stan ne tardera pas à revenir sur son avis.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Stop clopes : Les garçons sont surpris en train de fumer après avoir vu un spectacle ringard contre la cigarette. Rob Reiner, militant extrémiste anti-tabac, décide de piéger les fabricants de cigarettes avec l'aide des garçons.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Raisins : Stan déprime quand Wendy le plaque. Ses amis l'emmènent alors au 'Raisins', réputé pour ses hôtesses de bar sexy. Butters tombe amoureux de l'une d'entre elles, et Stan décide de devenir gothique.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Noël au Canada : Les parents biologiques d'Ike reviennent le chercher chez les parents de Kyle.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Les armes, c'est rigolo : Grâce à un stratagème surfait, les garçons obtiennent des armes ninja. Alors qu'ils jouent, ils blessent leur camarade Butters. Pris dans un dilemme draconien, les garçons décident de le déguiser en chien pour l'emmener chez le véterinaire.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Génial-O : Cartman s'habille comme un robot, s'auto-proclame 'Génial-O' et déménage chez Butters pour découvrir tous ses secrets et les utiliser contre lui.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Les stéroïdes ça déchire : Jimmy s'entraîne pour un événement sportif imminent et est déterminé à gagner à n'importe quel prix. Cartman décide qu'il peut facilement remporter la première place contre Jimmy.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: La passion du juif : Cartman a vu le film « La Passion du Christ ». Depuis, il est fan de Mel Gibson et de son film. Il veut alors monter un fan-club très spécial.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Les Gluants : Des humains de l'an 4035 débarquent à South Park à travers un portail temporel et recherchent du travail.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Les Jefferson : Une nouvelle famille arrive à South Park : les Jefferson. Monsieur Jefferson accueille les enfants de South Park chez lui pour les faire jouer dans son parc personnel.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Poire à lavement et sandwich au caca : La SPA manifeste contre l'utilisation d'une vache comme mascotte de l'école élémentaire de South Park. Les écoliers sont obligés d'en choisir une autre.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Le Supermarché des Ténèbres : La rue principale de South Park se transforme en ville fantôme quand un supermarché géant Wall Mart attire les citadins jusqu'à son tout nouveau magasin aux affaires incroyables.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Maternelle : Cela fait cinq ans que Trent Boyett est en prison. Celui-ci y est incarcéré, à la place des garçons, pour avoir brûlé leur professeur de maternelle. Quand il sort de prison, les enfants paniquent alors à l'idée de sa vengeance.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Course à l'audience : Les garçons de South Park produisent leur propre journal d'informations du matin sur la station de télévision de l'école, et sont immédiatement entraînés dans une intense course à l'audience.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Kit vidéo pour stupide pute trop gâtée : Paris Hilton ouvre une boutique en ville, dans laquelle toutes les petites filles pourront acheter de quoi se transformer en parfaite peste. Son chien s'étant suicidé, Paris veut acheter Butters pour le remplacer.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Le don incroyable de Cartman : Après avoir reçu une sévère blessure à la tête, Cartman se voit doté de dons de voyance.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Le Noël des petits animaux de la forêt : Stan est sollicité par un groupe d'adorables animaux de la forêt pour les aider à construire une crèche pour la naissance de leur Seigneur et sauveur. Il accepte, mais découvre qu'ils servent en fait Satan.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Le Vagin tout neuf de M. Garrison : Monsieur Garrison subit une opération de changement de sexe, mais il se rend compte qu'il ne pourra jamais connaître les plaisirs d'avoir ses règles ou l'avortement.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Crève hippie, crève : La ville de South Park est envahie par les hippies qui veulent faire un festival. Leur ennemi juré, Cartman, compte bien les arrêter.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Wing : Stan, Kyle, Cartman et Kenny décident de devenir imprésarios. Après l'échec de leur travail avec Token, on leur confie Wing, la femme du gérant de City Wok.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Potes pour la vie : Kenny obtient une PSP, mais il meurt. En réalité, Dieu l'a choisi pour mener une lutte salutaire au paradis. Malheureusement, les docteurs réaniment Kenny, qui se retrouve dans un état végétatif.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: La fureur de perdre : Les enfants de South Park sont désespérés : leurs parents veulent absolument qu'ils jouent au baseball mais eux s'ennuient terriblement. Tout empire lorsqu'ils apprennent qu'un match de qualification les obligerait à jouer tout l'été.

programme.title: Baggage
programme.desc: Leader of the Fanny Pack : Jerry Springer hosts as daters decide how much emotional "baggage" they can put up with to find love. A dater must choose between women whose baggage includes still sleeping with a baby blanket and always wearing a fanny pack.

programme.title: Baggage
programme.desc: Eat Your Heart Out : Jerry Springer hosts as daters decide how much emotional "baggage" they will put up with to find love. A dater must choose between women whose baggage includes having stolen $10,000 from her ex and making her boyfriend become vegan.

programme.title: Baggage
programme.desc: The Time is Meow : Jerry Springer hosts as daters decide how much emotional "baggage" they will put up with to find love. A dater must choose between women whose baggage includes refusing to do her hair or makeup and giving her cats spa treatments.

programme.title: Friend or Foe?
programme.desc: Friend or Foe ? : A game of knowledge and trust where teams of players must decide how they want to divide up their winnings.

programme.title: Divided
programme.desc: Team Effort : This unique new game show is as much about convincing others as it is about knowing the right answers. Four strangers including a personal trainer and a bartender must debate and unanimously agree against a racing clock. Mike Richards hosts.

programme.title: Divided
programme.desc: For The Glory : A unique game show which is as much about convincing others as it is about knowing the right answers. Four strangers including a fashion buyer and a tour guide must debate and unanimously agree against a racing clock. Mike Richards hosts.

programme.title: 1 vs 100
programme.desc: Episode 13 : Carrie Ann Inaba hosts this game where one player ("The One") is pitted against "The Mob" of 100 people, with each trying to correctly answer a series of multiple-choice trivia questions for a potential jackpot of $50,000.

programme.title: Best Ever Trivia Show
programme.desc: Flattery Will Get You Everywhere : Watch as three people enter a fun-filled showdown with a panel of Trivia Experts in the hopes of joining their elite ranks. If they know enough about Triple Crown-winning horses they just might! Sherri Shepherd hosts.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Constructive Criticism : In 2000, construction manager Darrell North was found dead in his work trailer in Ft. Worth, Texas. When investigators arrived, they found that he had been stabbed 46 times, indicating a personal relationship between the victim and the attacker.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Step by Step : When a wealthy socialite died after falling down the stairs, witness accounts contradicted evidence. To find out what really happened, investigators employed a physicist and an expert in accident reconstruction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Catch 22 : In 1996, 54-year-old Gayle Isleib was ambushed in her Manchester, Connecticut driveway and shot to death. Police had to determine whether obsessive love was the motive for her murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Partners in Crime : In a 1992 murder case, the autopsy of a cremated body turns up telltale clues of foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Scout's Honor : In 1984, a passerby finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. A forensic entomologist analyzes nearby insect activity to determine when the victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Killer's Cattle Log : When police in the Great Plains are called about a dead body, a background check on the victim leads them into a bizarre web of homeless drifters, cattle auctions and bad checks — all fronted by elderly couple Ray and Faye Copeland.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Woman Scorned : When Joann Katrinak is found dead in a corn field with her infant son, the obvious suspect is the husband and father of the victims. But insects found on the bodies reveal a vital clue which uncovers a twisted story of passion gone wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Sign of the Zodiac : For six years, a serial killer prowled the streets of New York City. He wrote letters to police and The New York Post, indicating he would kill twelve people — one for each astrological sign.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Point of Origin : In 1984, California firefighters battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the case to similar fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Dirty Laundry : The victim was a self-made man who never minced words. Perhaps he was murdered by a disgruntled client or employee. Perhaps the racy photos in his safe held the clue to solving the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Core Evidence : In 1996, more than a dozen children in Seattle, Washington, were fighting for their lives. Each one of them had contracted a serious unknown illness. After one died, investigators knew the clock was ticking.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Charred Remains : Shortly after daybreak, a fire is started in a dumpster in Vancouver. No one sees the arsonist or the fire, which burns for hours in the deserted parking lot. But there's more than garbage in this flaming container.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Blanket of Evidence : In 1997, Kelly Eckart's car was found, with her belongings still in it, after she worked a late shift in Franklin, Indiana. Days later, her body was found in an isolated ravine.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Leg Up On Crime : The decomposed body of a young woman was discovered in a Bakersfield irrigation canal. If there was trace evidence, it had been washed away.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Hair of the Dog : Investigators are baffled after finding a murder scene devoid of any physical evidence, but they suspect that the victim's dog had witnessed the crime. If so, forensic scientists would have to find some way to learn what the dog had seen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Treads and Threads : For 15 months, a serial killer was strangling prostitutes in Florida, then taunting police by leaving the bodies in plain sight. The only clues were a tire impression and some threads.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Flower Power : Two different men called police to report the same murder. Apparently, neither one knew that the other had called. The investigation uncovered even more unusual circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The Stake-Out : Police are led on a wild goose chase when the wife of a prominent banker is kidnapped. An entomologist's unexpected discovery leads to a new suspect — and the realization that ordinary office equipment can leave extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Foundation of Lies : Police suspect Dr. John "Jack" Boyle in the disappearance of his wife, Noreen, when he signs mortgage papers for a new house with a woman pretending to be his spouse. The police later find Noreen's body in the basement of Dr. Boyle's new house.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Critical Maneuver : When a hit-and-run accident claimed the life of a high school athlete, everyone in town mourned his passing. Finding the killer was a long shot at best.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Traces of Truth : A high school gym teacher mysteriously left town without saying goodbye to anyone. He later sent letters to explain why. But a closer forensic look at those letters gave investigators an entirely different explanation for where he went and why.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Key Evidence : When an off-duty policeman was shot dead, his fellow officers were determined to solve the crime. They needed clues to find the killer and they discovered them in tiny fibres and an asthma inhaler.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Breaking News : A young TV news producer is raped and murdered in her apartment. Police identify two suspects, but both are cleared of any wrongdoing. When the case stalls for more than a year, investigators turn to the Commonwealth of Virginia's DNA Databank.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Novel Idea : When the number and volume of bloodstains at the scene was greater than usual, it was up to forensic scientists to find out why.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Going for Broke : When a successful stockbroker doesn't return home after a late shift, his wife reports him missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: High 'n Dry : When Genell Plude is found dead in her bathroom, the scene points to suicide. But a coroner's inquest and a unique application of forensic science give investigators a different explanation for her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Dockter Visit : An investigation into the murder of a World War II veteran slowed to a halt when the prime suspect had a solid alibi. But a lucky break led to a shady character who wore distinctive boots and had a sweet tooth.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Trail of Truth : Nancy Newman and her two daughters are found brutally murdered. The crime scene has no shortage of evidence, but investigators cannot find a conclusive link to their prime suspect. To solve the puzzle, an FBI analyst designs a unique experiment.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The Common Thread : After 10 bodies turn up in Florida, one victim — 17-year-old Lisa McVey — is abducted but released. She leads police to the perpertrator, Bobbie Joe Long.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Seeing Red : In 2005, the body of young mother Summer Baldwin was found in a suitcase in a Texas landfill.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Root of All Evil : A suspect's former lover comes forward with a fantastic tale of murder, mutilation and cremation. But there's no way to test the story's validity — until a plant pathologist and a dendrochronologist conduct tests where the cremation supposedly occurred.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Fire Proof : A serial arsonist starts several fires that cause millions of dollars worth of damage and the deaths of three people. He is discovered, partly through the use of forensic hypnosis.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Traffic Violations : The body of a young woman was found a mile from her abandoned car. Police were especially concerned when they realized the victim had come to them for protection just two weeks earlier after a road rage incident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Pressed for Crime : Several suspects and conflicting evidence complicated a brutal murder investigation. But the forensics were clear on one thing: the killer knew his victim, and that alone gave investigators a head start.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Photo Finish : Professional photographer Charles Rathbun claims model Linda Sobek died during a consensual sexual encounter gone wrong, but Sobek's corpse and digital imaging tell a more sinister story.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Temptation : In June 2007, Dewayne Barrentine met Tausha Fields and soon after they began living together. Tausha told him some unbelievable stories about herself – leading Barrentine to verify her stories online, which resulted in the uncovering of a homicide.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Death Without Mercy : On November 29, 2005, firemen put out a fire in a house in Morgantown, West Virginia -- and discovered the charred remains of Jimmy Michael and his wife Shelly. A medical examiner’s report found that Jimmy was dead before the blaze had even started.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Deadly Proposal : Stephanie Pepper Sims was a college professor in the prime of her life when she suddenly vanished without a trace. Estranged from her husband, Stephanie moved in with a lover. Eventually, a hand-drawn map would lead investigators her body.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Chasing the Barefoot Bandit : A report on Colton Harris-Moore, also known as the “Barefoot Bandit,” because of his habit of committing crimes barefoot and leaving chalk outlines of his feet; and whose exploits included stealing planes, guns, yachts, and escaping to foreign countries.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Guilty Until Proven Innocent : Two men convicted for a brutal rape they say they didn’t commit, and DNA evidence backs them up. How did a law professor, her students and new DNA technology play a part in the case?

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Murder on His Mind : Stephen Stanko murdered his girlfriend Laura Ling, and then raped and stabbed her daughter. Stanko’s defense was he had a brain defect and was temporarily insane.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Fight For The Truth : 17-year-old Marty Tankleff was convicted of murdering his adoptive parents in their home. Marty initially confessed, but quickly recanted. He has fought for years for his innocence, as new witnesses have surfaced who could potentially exonerate him.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: The Secret Life of Eric Wright : In 1980, Kathi Spiars met a man who called himself Steve Marcum, and shortly married him.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: The Enemy Within : An investigation of the murder of Vicki Robinson, a 49-year-old divorced Florida real estate agent, and the role of her 15-year-old daughter, Valessa in the crime.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Deadly Prophecy : 48 Hours is television's most popular true-crime series, investigating shocking cases and compelling real-life dramas with journalistic integrity and cutting-edge style.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Lady in the Pool : In July 2008, Michael Roseboro called 911 to report that he had just found the body of his wife, Jan, at the bottom of their pool. An autopsy revealed that she had been strangled, beaten and then drowned: her death was ruled a homicide.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: The Year We Disappeared : In August 1979, John Busby, a Falmouth, Massachusetts police officer, was ambushed in his car by a gunman in a passing car who shot him in the face. Although wounded, Busby was able to write a note for police at the scene that said “Not an accident.”

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: The Negotiators : An inside look at 23-year-old Jarrett Jordan, a disturbed man who shot three people and barricaded himself in a house in Queens, NY. He had with him his four-month-old daughter and three hostages.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Thou Shalt Not Kill : A report on the November 1994 murder of Farah Fratta, whose body was discovered in the garage of her Humble, Texas home. She had been shot twice in the head.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Every Picture Tells A Story : On February 15, 2009, Cathy McNaughton was stabbed to death in her home in Sharpsburg, Georgia. The day had started with her husband Alec serving her breakfast in bed.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Blood in the Sand : DNA on one of two teenage girls killed in a similar fashion on a California beach six years apart leads investigators to a suspect who worked inside the police department.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Murder in L.A. : Focuses on two California couples: the Adairs and the Shapiros. Mindy Shapiro discovered that her husband Michael, an orthopedic surgeon, was having an affair with Jeanie Adair, and in 1996, after 11 years of marriage, the couple opted for divorce.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Live to Tell: Afraid of the Dark : A woman spends almost two decades searching for the man who kidnapped her from her bedroom and left her for dead when she was 8 years old.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Web of Seduction : A report on problems which arise when children under the age of 17 receive unwanted sexual letters on the Internet, focusing on Lindsay, a 14-year-old suburban Florida girl who began receiving explicit love letters from a stranger in Greece.
